title: Kiwanis Memorial Preserve Park - Parks | seattle.gov
content: # Kiwanis Memorial Preserve Park. 4441 Brygger Dr. W, Seattle, WA 98199. Phone: (206) 684-4075 684-4075). Walkway through Kiwanis Memorial Preserve Park - photo by TIA International Photography. Train tracks near Kiwanis Memorial Preserve Park - photo by TIA International Photography. Bridge in Kiwanis Memorial Preserve Park - photo by TIA International Photography. Greenspace in Kiwanis Memorial Preserve Park - photo by TIA International Photography. Kiwanis Memorial Preserve Park is one block east of Discovery Park in the Magnolia neighborhood. This park was named the city's first Wildlife Sanctuary in 2010. It was the home to Seattle's largest nesting colony of Great Blue Herons. In May, 2013, due to extreme eagle predation, the Kiwanis Ravine heron colony moved to Commodore Park. The public can enter shoulder areas of the ravine during daylight hours year-round. Mailing Address: 100 Dexter Ave N, Seattle, WA, 98109. Fax: (206) 615-1813 615-1813). Email: pks\_info@seattle.gov. 4. Find a Restroom in a Park.
